content.text: RBAC is in place. Now I'll deploy the headless service, public service, PodDisruptionBudget, and StatefulSet.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr
        env:
        - name: COCKROACH_CHANNEL
          value: kubernetes-insecure
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Deploy CockroachDB services, PDB, and StatefulSet

content.text: Resources created. Now polling until all 3 pods are Running and Ready.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 36); do
  echo "--- attempt $i ---"
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb 2>&1
  READY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "1/1.*Running" || true)
  echo "Ready pods: $READY/3"
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods ready."
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done

content.thinking:
Only `crdb-cluster-0` is coming up, and `crdb-cluster-1` and `crdb-cluster-2` haven't even started. That's because the StatefulSet creates pods sequentially by default - each pod must be Ready before the next is created. But `crdb-cluster-0` is stuck at `0/1 Running` - not Ready. This is because CockroachDB in cluster mode needs `cockroach init` to be run after the first node starts, before it becomes Ready.

Wait, actually, CockroachDB pods waiting on `--join` are stuck because they're waiting for quorum - they need a `cockroach init` to be run to initialize the cluster. 

The pods should actually start and run even without initialization, they just won't accept SQL connections. The readiness probe is what's blocking - it checks `/health?ready=1` which fails until the cluster is initialized. The real problem is that `OrderedReady` pod management policy won't start the next pod until the previous one passes its readiness check, creating a deadlock where `crdb-cluster-0` can't become Ready without initialization, but we can't initialize without the other nodes joining. I need to switch to `Parallel` pod management so all nodes start simultaneously.

content.text: `crdb-cluster-0` is stuck not-Ready because CockroachDB needs `cockroach init` before the readiness probe passes, and with `OrderedReady` pod management the other pods won't start until pod-0 is Ready. I need to switch to `Parallel` pod management, get all nodes running, then initialize the cluster.

input.description: Patch StatefulSet to use Parallel pod management

content.content:
Exit code 1
The StatefulSet "crdb-cluster" is invalid: spec: Forbidden: updates to statefulset spec for fields other than 'replicas', 'ordinals', 'template', 'updateStrategy', 'persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy' and 'minReadySeconds' are forbidden

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 1
The StatefulSet "crdb-cluster" is invalid: spec: Forbidden: updates to statefulset spec for fields other than 'replicas', 'ordinals', 'template', 'updateStrategy', 'persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy' and 'minReadySeconds' are forbidden

content.thinking:
I need to delete and recreate the StatefulSet with `Parallel` pod management. I'll keep the PVCs though - actually I need to delete the StatefulSet but keep the PVCs (which is the default behavior when you delete a StatefulSet - PVCs are retained).

Wait, actually the PVC hasn't been claimed yet for pods 1 and 2 since those pods haven't started. Let me delete the StatefulSet (keeping PVCs), then recreate with Parallel pod management.

Actually, I should delete with `--cascade=orphan` to keep the pods too, then recreate. 

But it's simpler to just delete the StatefulSet outright—the pods will be removed but the PVCs from the volumeClaimTemplates will stick around. Then I'll recreate it with Parallel pod management and run cockroach init.

content.text: Can't patch `podManagementPolicy` in-place. I'll delete and recreate the StatefulSet with `Parallel` mode, then run `cockroach init`.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb delete statefulset crdb-cluster --cascade=foreground 2>&1
echo "exit: $?"
input.description: Delete StatefulSet (foreground, pods terminate before returning)

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr
        env:
        - name: COCKROACH_CHANNEL
          value: kubernetes-insecure
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Recreate StatefulSet with Parallel pod management

input.command:
# Wait for all 3 pods to be Running (not necessarily Ready yet — init hasn't run)
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  RUNNING=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)
  echo "attempt $i: $RUNNING/3 pods Running"
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null
  if [ "$RUNNING" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods are Running — ready to init."
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
